message:
mt-wasm: default thread count from 4 to min(6, cores-1)

Sweep on M-series shows 6 threads beat 4 threads on every input
size (T=32: 93→76 ms, T=128: 315→258 ms — ~20% improvement). 8
threads regresses, likely from worker threads spilling onto
E-cores while the main thread gets pre-empted. Capping at
`hardwareConcurrency - 1` keeps one core for main thread + non-
worker JS work. Capping at 6 prevents over-spawning on big-iron
machines (16+ cores) where dispatch overhead would dominate.

Default speedup vs single-thread WASM at T=32 jumps from 2.52x
(4-thread default) to 3.08x (6-thread default).

Browser smoke + webgpu-smoke still produce 2/2 agreed spans.
